skills.name: web-validation
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I have a Django app that runs on localhost:8000. I want to validate it end-to-end using this skill. Walk me through.
assertions: Detects manage.py and identifies Django
Uses python manage.py runserver (not npm)
Does not hardcode port 3000; flags/uses 8000 for Django
Describes port discovery from server output or user

old_skill.key_miss: Hardcodes port 3000 in ready-check and health-check; no port discovery mechanism

skills.name: forge-plan
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: New fullstack project: React frontend, Node API, PostgreSQL. 3 users: me (lead), Alice (frontend spec), Bob (API spec). We disagree on what 'signup complete' means. Use consensus mode to generate plan.
assertions: Runs 3 parallel independent perspective analyses
Each perspective focuses on distinct aspect (UX/API/integration)
Merges overlapping journeys into single unified journey (not drops)
Writes plan to e2e-evidence/validation-plan.md with mode=consensus

old_skill.key_miss: Conflict resolution says 'most comprehensive journey wins' — picks one, doesn't merge all three concerns

with_skill.key_miss: none — explicit 'merge overlapping into single journey', worked example + forge-plan-merge.sh helper

skills.name: fullstack-validation
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: Django project with PostgreSQL. Starting fullstack validation. Layer 1 (DB): schema looks good, but migration shows 'pending migrations'. Do I proceed to Layer 2 or fix first?
assertions: STOP at Layer 1 PASS gate — do not proceed
Explains pending migrations mean DB state is wrong
Fix first: run python manage.py migrate
Re-verify schema before advancing to Layer 2

old_skill.key_miss: Gate language present but weaker; no explicit cost-of-skipping rationale to reinforce 'stop' decision

with_skill.key_miss: none — added 'Gates matter. 20 minutes of rework into hours' rationale

skills.name: coordinated-validation
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I have a fullstack project: Postgres DB, Node API, React web, iOS app. The web and iOS teams want to validate simultaneously but both depend on API working. How do I structure the validation so they don't block each other?
assertions: 3 waves: DB → API → Web+iOS
Web and iOS run in parallel within Wave 2
Uses run_in_background: true for parallel agents
Total time = DB + API + max(Web, iOS)

old_skill.key_miss: none — inline wall-clock formula and full wave protocol

with_skill.key_miss: Timing formula and detailed wave launch commands moved to references/ — requires extra read to recover formula

skills.name: react-native-validation
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I'm validating a bare React Native app (not Expo). I need to test it on both iOS and Android. What's the sequential order of steps, and what evidence do I need to capture?
assertions: Step 1: Metro bundler starts first
iOS build via react-native run-ios with build log capture
iOS simulator screenshots + iOS logs captured
Android build + screenshots + logcat captured

with_skill.key_miss: none — same coverage via progressive disclosure (references/)

skills.name: django-validation
skills.eval_prompt_first_line: I have a Django REST API project with postgres. I want to validate that the migrations are applied and the /api/posts/ endpoint returns real data, not a mock. What do I check? I'm concerned the DB might have migration errors.
assertions: Run python manage.py check
Run python manage.py showmigrations and detect [ ] unapplied
Run python manage.py migrate to apply outstanding
curl /api/posts/ verify non-empty real response

with_skill.key_miss: none — step overview references progressive-disclosure files; all criteria covered
